mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.16% represents the zero lower bound constraint on monetary policy. When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, the Fed has exhausted conventional rate cuts and must rely on quantitative easing and forward guidance. This signals maximum monetary accommodation, which reduces equity discount rates and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing at zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 131,223 thousand represents a stabilization point after the 2008-2009 crisis. When monthly PAYEMS growth turns positive (month-over-month increase), it signals the labor market has bottomed and the economy is entering recovery. This forward-looking signal of demand recovery drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 351,418 million dollars reflect consumer spending patterns. When RSAFS declines month-over-month, it signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations. This negative demand signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to lower near-term profit forecasts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 95.97 (2017=100) measures real economic output capacity. When INDPRO rises month-over-month, it signals expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side conditions. This supply expansion, combined with accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS near zero), creates conditions for positive equity returns as firms can increase output without inflation pressure over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2009-08-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 581 thousand units represent forward-looking demand for residential investment. When HOUST rises quarter-over-quarter, it signals improving housing demand and construction activity, which drives employment and consumer confidence. This demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained economic recovery.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average > previous 3-month average by > 2%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST growth and 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 9.7% is elevated but declining from crisis peaks. When UNRATE falls month-over-month by more than 0.1 percentage points, it signals accelerating labor market improvement and rising consumer confidence. This labor market improvement, combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS), indicates robust demand recovery that drives positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month change < -0.1pp AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% and RSAFS at 351,418 million as of 2009-08-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term (UNRATE × RSAFS) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 214.47 (1982-1984=100) reflects year-over-year price changes. When CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month, it signals deflationary pressure that increases real interest rates despite low nominal rates. This deflationary signal, when FEDFUNDS is already near zero, creates a regime where real rates are rising, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.47 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2009-07-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 12,892.5 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the broadest measure of economic activity. When GDPC1 growth turns positive quarter-over-quarter (after contraction), it signals the economy has exited recession. This regime shift from contraction to expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity markets price in sustained recovery and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate > 0% after prior negative quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following positive GDPC1 growth inflection, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
